critic_raw:
This experiment tests a multimodal neural network (structured data + clinical text) versus logistic regression on structured features only for post-operative complication prediction within 90 days.

The primary metric is sensitivity at a fixed 85% specificity. Model selection on the validation set uses this same metric. Fixing the specificity at 85% during validation means the decision threshold is determined by the validation set's score distribution. When applied to the test set, the same threshold will produce a different specificity because the test set's score distribution may differ. The design says the threshold is 'locked before test evaluation,' but the actual test specificity will not be exactly 85% — it will vary depending on the test set's class distribution. Reporting 'sensitivity at 85% specificity' on the test set thus requires a second threshold search on the test set, or the metric reported will not correspond to 85% specificity. This is a circular evaluation design.

The multimodal model uses clinical text from 'preoperative assessment notes, operative reports, and intraoperative notes.' Intraoperative notes describe what happened during surgery and are written retrospectively. If any intraoperative notes contain language that reflects the surgeon's awareness of intraoperative complications or difficulties (e.g., 'difficult dissection,' 'bleeding encountered'), these notes may encode foreknowledge of post-operative risk. This is not conventional label leakage in the strict sense, but it creates a scenario where the model exploits surgeon-perceived difficulty at the time of writing rather than objective pre-surgery features.

Stratification by surgeon and complication rate quartile within each temporal window ensures balanced representation. However, the model also uses 'surgeon identifier (fixed effect)' as a feature. If the surgeon encoding in the model includes a surgeon-specific fixed effect that is learned from training data, and new surgeons join or existing surgeons retire during the 5-year study period, some surgeons in the validation/test sets will be unseen during training. The one-hot encoding of surgeon identifier will produce an 'other' or zero vector for unseen surgeons, which the model has not been trained to handle. No out-of-vocabulary surgeon handling strategy is described.

The baseline does not use text embeddings, and the comparison evaluates the contribution of multimodal integration. However, the baseline also excludes the neural network's capacity advantage (the multimodal architecture has substantially more parameters). A neural network on structured features only (without text) would be the appropriate capacity-matched control to separate the information effect from the architectural effect.
all_issues_raised: The decision threshold locked at validation 85% specificity will not produce exactly 85% specificity on the test set due to score distribution differences. Reporting test sensitivity at 85% specificity either requires an additional threshold calibration on the test set (inflating metrics) or is simply inaccurate.

Intraoperative notes are written retrospectively during or after surgery and may reflect surgeon-perceived intraoperative difficulty, encoding foreknowledge of post-operative risk rather than purely pre-surgery features.

Surgeon identifier used as a fixed effect feature will encounter out-of-vocabulary surgeons in the test set (new surgeons, departures). No strategy for handling unseen surgeons is described, and the model will produce unvalidated representations for these cases.

The baseline is logistic regression without text, while the proposed model is a neural network with text. No capacity-matched neural control on structured features only is included, so architectural capacity and information access are simultaneously varied in the comparison.
all_issues_adjudicated: The decision threshold locked at validation 85% specificity will not produce exactly 85% specificity on the test set due to score distribution differences. Reporting test sensitivity at 85% specificity either requires an additional threshold calibration on the test set (inflating metrics) or is simply inaccurate.

Intraoperative notes are written retrospectively during or after surgery and may reflect surgeon-perceived intraoperative difficulty, encoding foreknowledge of post-operative risk rather than purely pre-surgery features.

Surgeon identifier used as a fixed effect feature will encounter out-of-vocabulary surgeons in the test set (new surgeons, departures). No strategy for handling unseen surgeons is described, and the model will produce unvalidated representations for these cases.

The baseline is logistic regression without text, while the proposed model is a neural network with text. No capacity-matched neural control on structured features only is included, so architectural capacity and information access are simultaneously varied in the comparison.
